guardrails: Lemma C (degree-<=2 bijection => one dependent side, dim D<=2) is PROVEN by a GL(4,2)-symmetry-complete finite check, atlas-independent.
Step5+ upgrades the dichotomy from 'verified over 7040 magic squares' to a proven property of quadratic bijections of F2^4; magic enters only via Lemma B (Step4/item1) and bijectivity.
The corner identity B11(+)B10(+)B01(+)B00=0 is a magic refinement, NOT part of Lemma C and NOT needed for the dichotomy.
No public/PAPP widening or universal-24 claim is made; the B3 multiplicity matrix remains a finite certificate.

metadata.description: Atlas-independent (delta, dim D) dichotomy: 'one dependent side' is a property of degree-<=2 bijections of F2^4 (Lemma C), not of the magic sums.

summary.corner_sum_zero_is_magic_not_bijection: B11(+)B10(+)B01(+)B00=0 holds on the atlas (corner xor 0) but is NOT a bijection consequence; some completable M have nonzero sum. It is a magic refinement, not part of Lemma C.
summary.lemma_c_proof_space: entries in <e0,e1,e2> (covers dim D<=3 up to GL(4,2)) + dim-4 basis orbit
summary.next_task: Item 3 closed. Optionally fold Lemma C into the paper as a GL(4,2)-symmetry-complete finite theorem, or proceed to another item of the non-enumerative programme.
summary.residual: Lemma C is now CLOSED as a finite theorem: a symmetry-complete check (codomain GL(4,2) transitive on subspaces) verifies that every degree-<=2 bijection has one dependent side and dim D<=2. Item 3 (the delta/dimD dichotomy) is therefore atlas-independent and proven. The B3 15/49 multiplicity matrix remains finite-certified (item 7).
